prompt: Which batter holds the record for the highest individual score in Test cricket, with 400 not out?
explanation: Brian Lara's unbeaten 400 against England in 2004 is the highest individual Test score ever.

prompt: Which batter held the Test record for highest individual score before Brian Lara reclaimed it in 2004, having also once held it himself?
options: Brian Lara (who scored 375 in 1994 before Hayden briefly surpassed it)
explanation: Lara's 375 in 1994 was later surpassed by Matthew Hayden's 380 in 2003, before Lara reclaimed the record with 400 in 2004.

prompt: Which Australian batter briefly held the Test record for highest individual score with 380 in 2003?
explanation: Hayden's 380 against Zimbabwe was the record for less than a year before Lara reclaimed it.

prompt: Which Indian batter scored a double century (219) in an ODI, setting a record at the time in 2011-12?
explanation: Sachin Tendulkar became the first man to score a double century in ODI cricket, against South Africa in 2010.

prompt: Which Indian batter holds the record for the highest individual score in an ODI innings, with 264?
explanation: Rohit Sharma's 264 against Sri Lanka in 2014 remains the highest individual ODI score.

prompt: Which West Indian batter smashed an unbeaten 175 off just 66 balls for Royal Challengers Bangalore in the 2013 IPL, one of the greatest T20 innings ever played?
explanation: Gayle's 175 not out remains one of the most explosive individual innings in T20 history.

prompt: Which bowler holds the record for most wickets in Test cricket history?
explanation: Muralitharan's 800 Test wickets is the all-time record.

prompt: Which Australian leg-spin bowler is second on the all-time Test wickets list, with 708?
explanation: Shane Warne revolutionized leg-spin bowling and remains one of the most celebrated bowlers ever.

prompt: Which English fast bowler became the leading wicket-taker among pace bowlers in Test history, with over 700 wickets?
explanation: James Anderson's longevity and skill made him England's greatest ever fast bowler by wicket count.

prompt: Which Indian leg-spin bowler is a member of the elite '600 Test wickets' club, famous for his variations and accuracy?
explanation: Anil Kumble's 619 Test wickets makes him India's leading wicket-taker in the format.

prompt: Which bowler took all 10 wickets in a single Test innings, a feat achieved only twice in Test history?
options: Both Kumble and Laker have done this
explanation: Jim Laker (1956) and Anil Kumble (1999) are the only two bowlers to take all 10 wickets in a Test innings.

prompt: Which English bowler was the first to take all 10 wickets in a Test innings, doing so against Australia in 1956?
explanation: Jim Laker's 10-wicket haul at Old Trafford remains one of the most famous bowling performances in history.

prompt: Which Australian fielder holds the record for most catches by a non-wicketkeeper in Test cricket, with 181?
explanation: Mark Waugh's 181 Test catches is the all-time record for a non-wicketkeeper.

prompt: Which Indian batter is renowned as one of the greatest slip fielders and holds a very high catch count for a non-wicketkeeper?
explanation: Rahul Dravid's reliability in the slips made him one of the greatest fielders of his era, alongside his batting excellence.

prompt: Which wicketkeeper holds the Test record for most dismissals (catches plus stumpings)?
explanation: South Africa's Mark Boucher recorded 555 Test dismissals, the most by any wicketkeeper.

prompt: Which Australian wicketkeeper revolutionized the role by combining elite keeping with explosive batting?
explanation: Gilchrist's aggressive batting from the wicketkeeper slot changed how the position was viewed.

prompt: Which Indian captain led the team to victory in both the 2007 T20 World Cup and the 2011 ODI World Cup?
explanation: Dhoni's captaincy brought India multiple ICC titles, including the 2013 Champions Trophy as well.

prompt: Which format did India win in 2007 under MS Dhoni's captaincy, their first ICC T20 title?
explanation: India won the inaugural T20 World Cup in South Africa in 2007, beating Pakistan in the final.

prompt: Which player hit the six that won India the 2011 Cricket World Cup final against Sri Lanka?
explanation: Dhoni's iconic six in the 2011 final remains one of the most celebrated moments in Indian cricket history.

prompt: Which player was named Player of the Tournament at the 2011 Cricket World Cup for his all-round performances?
explanation: Yuvraj Singh's batting and bowling contributions were vital to India's World Cup triumph in 2011.

prompt: Which country's captain, Kapil Dev, led an unfancied team to a shock 1983 Cricket World Cup title?
explanation: India's 1983 World Cup win under Kapil Dev was one of the biggest upsets in cricket history at the time.

prompt: Which team did India defeat in the final to win the 1983 Cricket World Cup?
explanation: India stunned the two-time defending champions West Indies in the final at Lord's.

prompt: Which team won the 1996 Cricket World Cup, hosted across the Indian subcontinent?
explanation: Sri Lanka's victory in 1996 was their first and only World Cup title, led by captain Arjuna Ranatunga.

prompt: Which team has won three consecutive Cricket World Cups from 1999 to 2007?
explanation: Australia's dominant run of three straight titles (1999, 2003, 2007) remains unmatched.

prompt: Which Australian captain led the team to the 1999 Cricket World Cup title, before Ricky Ponting took over and led the 2003 and 2007 triumphs?
explanation: Steve Waugh captained Australia's 1999 World Cup win, the first of their three consecutive titles.

prompt: Which format of cricket features a competition called the Champions Trophy, contested among top ODI teams?
explanation: The ICC Champions Trophy is an ODI tournament, sometimes dubbed a 'mini World Cup'.

prompt: Which country won the 2017 ICC Champions Trophy?
explanation: Pakistan defeated India in the final to win their first Champions Trophy title in 2017.

prompt: Which bowling figure describes a bowler taking 5 or more wickets in a single innings, a notable milestone?
explanation: Taking five wickets in an innings is a significant achievement for any bowler, informally called a 'fifer'.

prompt: What is the term for a batter's dismissal that occurs when the umpire rules them out for deliberately obstructing the fielding side?
options: Handled the ball (a separate, related law)
explanation: Obstructing the field is a rare dismissal method for deliberately hindering fielders.

prompt: Which Pakistani fast bowler pioneered 'reverse swing' and is considered one of the greatest fast bowlers of all time?
explanation: Wasim Akram, nicknamed the 'Sultan of Swing', mastered reverse swing alongside his new-ball partner Waqar Younis.

prompt: Which Pakistani fast bowler, known as the 'Rawalpindi Express', holds the record for the fastest recorded delivery in cricket history?
explanation: Shoaib Akhtar bowled a delivery recorded at 161.3 km/h in the 2003 World Cup, the fastest ever recorded.

prompt: Which Pakistani all-rounder captained the team to their first Cricket World Cup title in 1992?
explanation: Imran Khan led Pakistan to their only Cricket World Cup title, defeating England in the final.

prompt: Which South African all-rounder is regarded as one of the greatest of all time, excelling with bat, ball, and in the field?
explanation: Kallis's combination of over 13,000 Test runs and 292 Test wickets is virtually unmatched.

prompt: Which South African batter, nicknamed 'Mr. 360', was famous for his ability to score in all directions around the ground?
explanation: De Villiers's innovative shot-making earned him the nickname 'Mr. 360'.

prompt: Which New Zealand captain led the team to the 2021 ICC World Test Championship title?
explanation: Williamson led New Zealand to victory over India in the inaugural World Test Championship final.

prompt: Which tournament, first held in 2019-21, crowns the top Test-playing nation over a two-year cycle?
explanation: The World Test Championship was introduced to give context and a defined 'champion' to Test cricket.

prompt: Which trophy is contested between India and Australia in Test cricket?
explanation: Named after Allan Border and Sunil Gavaskar, this trophy has become one of Test cricket's premier rivalries.

prompt: Which trophy is contested between the West Indies and Australia in Test cricket?
explanation: Named after West Indian great Frank Worrell, this trophy has a long Test history between the sides.

prompt: Which format of the game features 'The Hundred', an English tournament using a novel ball-counting structure?
explanation: The Hundred uses sets of five balls instead of traditional six-ball overs, totaling 100 balls per innings.

prompt: Which West Indian batter is known for his aggressive, powerful hitting and multiple IPL centuries, nicknamed the 'Universe Boss'?
explanation: Chris Gayle's explosive batting made him one of the most feared T20 hitters in history.

prompt: Which Sri Lankan batter is famous for reinventing the opening batter's role with ultra-aggressive strokeplay in the 1996 World Cup?
explanation: Jayasuriya's fearless approach at the top of the order helped revolutionize ODI batting tactics.

prompt: Which Sri Lankan batter was named Player of the Tournament at the 1996 Cricket World Cup?
explanation: De Silva's brilliant all-round performances, including a match-winning century in the final, secured the award.

prompt: Which Bangladeshi all-rounder has been one of the most consistent performers in ODI cricket for his country?
explanation: Shakib Al Hasan has long been ranked among the top all-rounders in world cricket across formats.

prompt: Which format of cricket introduced the Decision Review System (DRS), allowing teams to challenge umpiring decisions?
options: It was first introduced in Test cricket in 2008
It has never been used in Test cricket
explanation: DRS was first trialed in a Test series between India and Sri Lanka in 2008 before wider adoption.

prompt: What technology, used within DRS, tracks the ball's flight path to determine if it would have hit the stumps for LBW decisions?
explanation: Hawk-Eye uses multiple cameras to predict ball trajectory for LBW reviews.

prompt: What technology, used within DRS, uses infrared imaging to detect faint contact between bat and ball?
explanation: Hot Spot highlights areas of friction, helping determine whether the ball made contact with the bat.

statement: Brian Lara holds the record for the highest individual score in Test cricket, with 400 not out.
explanation: Lara's unbeaten 400 against England in 2004 remains the Test record.

statement: Muttiah Muralitharan holds the record for most wickets in Test cricket history.
explanation: Muralitharan's 800 wickets is the all-time Test record.

statement: Jim Laker and Anil Kumble are the only two bowlers to take all 10 wickets in a single Test innings.
explanation: This rare feat has only been achieved twice in Test history.

statement: MS Dhoni captained India to victory in both the 2007 T20 World Cup and the 2011 ODI World Cup.
explanation: Dhoni's captaincy delivered multiple major ICC titles for India.

statement: Kapil Dev captained India to their first Cricket World Cup title in 1983.
explanation: India's stunning 1983 triumph is one of the greatest upsets in cricket history.

statement: Australia has won three consecutive Cricket World Cups, from 1999 to 2007.
explanation: This remains an unmatched run of dominance in World Cup history.

statement: Shoaib Akhtar holds the record for the fastest recorded delivery in cricket history.
explanation: His delivery at 161.3 km/h during the 2003 World Cup remains unmatched.

statement: Rohit Sharma holds the record for the highest individual score in an ODI innings, with 264.
explanation: Sharma's 264 against Sri Lanka in 2014 is the ODI record.

statement: Imran Khan captained Pakistan to their first and only Cricket World Cup title in 1992.
explanation: Pakistan defeated England in the final under Khan's leadership.

statement: The ICC World Test Championship crowns a champion based on a single calendar year of results.
explanation: The World Test Championship uses a two-year cycle culminating in a final between the top two teams.

clues: Holds the record for highest individual score in Test cricket (400 not out)
Also once held the previous record of 375
explanation: Lara's twin record-breaking innings against England cemented his legacy as one of the greatest batters ever.

clues: Holds the record for most Test wickets in history
Famous for his unique bowling action
explanation: Muralitharan's wicket-taking prowess made him one of the most dominant bowlers cricket has ever seen.

clues: Led India to the 2007 T20 World Cup and 2011 ODI World Cup titles
Famous for his calm demeanor, nicknamed 'Captain Cool'
Hit the winning six in the 2011 World Cup final
explanation: Dhoni's captaincy and clutch performances made him one of India's most beloved cricketers.

clues: Captained Pakistan and mentored a generation of fast bowlers
explanation: Akram's mastery of swing bowling, especially reverse swing, revolutionized fast bowling tactics.

prompt: Order these Test cricket highest individual score records chronologically by when they were set (earliest first).
events: Brian Lara reclaims the record with 400 not out (2004)
This record still stands as of the mid-2020s
explanation: Order: Lara's 375 in 1994, Hayden's 380 in 2003, Lara's 400 in 2004, standing since.

prompt: Which format of cricket does the 'Mankad' method of dismissal (running out the non-striker backing up too far) most commonly generate controversy in?
options: All formats, though it draws particular scrutiny in white-ball cricket
It's illegal in all forms of cricket
It only applies in Test cricket
explanation: Mankading is legal under the laws of cricket but remains controversial regardless of format, especially in high-stakes white-ball games.

prompt: After which Indian bowler is the controversial 'Mankad' dismissal named, having first used it in 1947?
explanation: Vinoo Mankad ran out Bill Brown of Australia in this manner in 1947, giving the dismissal its name.

prompt: Which England captain led the team to their maiden T20 World Cup title in 2010?
explanation: Collingwood led England to their first global T20 title, beating Australia in the final in the Caribbean.

prompt: Which England captain led the team to the 2019 Cricket World Cup and the 2022 T20 World Cup titles?
explanation: Morgan captained England to their first-ever ODI World Cup in 2019 and their second T20 World Cup crown in 2022.

prompt: Which England all-rounder was named Player of the Match in both the 2019 World Cup final and later led the Test team as captain?
explanation: Stokes's heroics in the 2019 final, followed by his Test captaincy, made him one of England's most influential cricketers.

prompt: Which unusual rule decided the outcome of the tied 2019 Cricket World Cup final after a tied Super Over?
options: England won on boundary count (having hit more fours and sixes)
explanation: With both the match and the Super Over tied, England were declared winners for hitting more boundaries throughout the innings.

prompt: Which format of dismissal occurs when a batter is out having 'handled the ball' deliberately, a law later merged into obstructing the field?
explanation: This dismissal, once a standalone law, was folded into 'obstructing the field' in the 2017 MCC Laws update.

prompt: Which format of dismissal occurs when an incoming batter fails to be ready to face a ball within a specified time limit, usually 3 minutes?
explanation: 'Timed out' is an extremely rare dismissal for batters who take too long to reach the crease.

prompt: Which Indian bowler is renowned for his 'doosra' delivery, a variation that spins the opposite way to a traditional off-break?
explanation: The doosra, popularized by bowlers like Harbhajan Singh and Saqlain Mushtaq, deceives batters expecting standard spin.

prompt: Which Pakistani bowler is often credited with inventing the 'doosra' delivery in the 1990s?
explanation: Saqlain Mushtaq is widely credited as the pioneer of the doosra, a mystery spin variation.

prompt: Which Pakistani leg-spinner of the 1980s is considered one of the pioneers of modern leg-spin bowling, inspiring Shane Warne?
explanation: Abdul Qadir kept the art of leg-spin alive during an era dominated by pace bowling.

prompt: Which Indian left-arm wrist-spinner became a key figure in white-ball cricket in the 2010s, known for his 'chinaman' bowling style?
explanation: Kuldeep Yadav's unusual left-arm wrist-spin style made him a valuable wicket-taking option for India.

prompt: What is the informal cricket term for a left-arm unorthodox spin bowler, derived from a historical (now considered outdated) reference?
options: Wrist-spinner (generic term, not specific to left-arm)
explanation: 'Chinaman' describes a left-arm wrist-spinner, though the term's origins are considered controversial by some today.

prompt: Which format of the Cricket World Cup featured exactly 10 teams competing in a round-robin followed by semifinals, used in both 2019 and 2023?
options: A single round-robin group stage with all teams playing each other
A league format followed by direct final
Group stage with only 6 teams advancing
explanation: Both the 2019 and 2023 World Cups used a single round-robin format among all participating teams before the semifinals.

prompt: Which country hosted the 2015 Cricket World Cup, co-hosting alongside New Zealand?
explanation: Australia and New Zealand jointly hosted the 2015 tournament, which Australia went on to win.

prompt: Which team won the 2015 Cricket World Cup, defeating New Zealand in the final on home soil?
explanation: Australia claimed a record-tying fifth World Cup title in front of a home crowd in Melbourne.

prompt: Which team lost consecutive Cricket World Cup finals in 2015 and 2019 without winning the title?
explanation: New Zealand's back-to-back final losses in 2015 and 2019 were painful near-misses for the team.

prompt: Which South African team is often associated with a reputation for underperforming in ICC knockout matches, dubbed 'chokers' by fans and media?
explanation: South Africa's history of falling short in crucial ICC tournament moments led to this unflattering nickname, though they reached the 2024 T20 World Cup final.

prompt: Which team did South Africa lose to in the final of the 2024 T20 World Cup, narrowly missing their first ICC title?
explanation: India won a tense final by 7 runs, denying South Africa their first major ICC trophy.

prompt: Which Indian all-rounder bowled the tense final over to seal India's win in the 2024 T20 World Cup final against South Africa?
explanation: Hardik Pandya defended a tight target in the final over to secure India's title in Barbados.

prompt: Which Indian bowler was named Player of the Tournament at the 2024 T20 World Cup for his exceptional bowling throughout?
explanation: Bumrah's economy and wicket-taking were central to India's title-winning campaign.

prompt: Which Indian batter was named Player of the Match in the 2024 T20 World Cup final for his crucial innings?
explanation: Kohli's 76 in the final was pivotal in setting up India's title-winning total.

prompt: Which fielder took a stunning boundary-line catch to dismiss David Miller in the closing stages of the 2024 T20 World Cup final?
explanation: Suryakumar Yadav's remarkable catch on the rope proved a turning point in the final.

prompt: Which Indian captain led the team to the 2024 T20 World Cup title, his final match in the format before retiring from T20Is?
explanation: Rohit Sharma retired from T20 internationals immediately after lifting the trophy in Barbados.

prompt: Which format of bowling figure describes an economy rate, and what does a lower number generally indicate?
options: Runs conceded per over — a lower economy rate indicates more effective, tighter bowling
Wickets taken per over — higher is better
Balls bowled per wicket — lower is worse
Total runs scored by a bowler while batting
explanation: Economy rate measures how many runs a bowler concedes per over on average; lower is generally better for the bowling side.

prompt: What statistic describes the average number of runs a batter scores per dismissal across their career?
explanation: Batting average is calculated as total runs scored divided by number of times dismissed.

prompt: What statistic measures how quickly a batter scores runs, expressed as runs per 100 balls faced?
explanation: Strike rate is especially important in limited-overs cricket, reflecting scoring speed.

prompt: What statistic measures a team's average scoring pace in an innings, expressed as runs per over?
explanation: Run rate reflects how quickly a team is scoring across an innings, crucial in run-chases.

prompt: Which target-setting method is used to fairly adjust scores in rain-affected limited-overs matches?
explanation: The DLS method recalculates a fair target when overs are lost due to weather or other interruptions.

prompt: Which statistic is used to separate teams tied on points in group-stage standings during limited-overs tournaments?
explanation: Net Run Rate compares the rate at which teams score runs versus the rate they concede, used as a key tiebreaker.

prompt: Which format of over-rate penalty affects teams that fail to bowl their overs within the allotted time in limited-overs cricket?
options: Fielding restrictions, such as an extra fielder inside the circle for the batting side
explanation: Slow over rates can result in fielding restrictions being imposed as a penalty on the offending team.

prompt: What is the term for the area within 30 yards of the pitch, inside which a minimum number of fielders must be stationed during certain phases of a limited-overs innings?
explanation: Fielding restrictions require a set number of fielders inside the 30-yard circle during specific overs, like the powerplay.

prompt: What is the term for the opening overs of a limited-overs innings, during which fielding restrictions are typically most relaxed for the batting side?
explanation: The powerplay period restricts the number of fielders allowed outside the inner circle, favoring batters.

prompt: What is the term for the final overs of a limited-overs innings, typically overs 16-20 in a T20 or 41-50 in an ODI, when scoring rates often accelerate?
explanation: Death overs are the tense closing stages of an innings, where both risk and required run rate typically increase.

prompt: What is awarded to the batting side after a no-ball is bowled in limited-overs cricket, allowing a free swing without risk of being out (except by run out)?
explanation: A free hit follows certain no-balls, letting the batter attempt a big shot without fear of most dismissal types.

statement: Kane Williamson captained New Zealand to victory in the inaugural ICC World Test Championship final in 2021.
explanation: New Zealand beat India in the final held in Southampton to claim the first WTC title.

statement: Rohit Sharma retired from T20 international cricket immediately after India's 2024 T20 World Cup win.
explanation: Rohit announced his T20I retirement in the aftermath of India's title celebration.

statement: The Duckworth-Lewis-Stern (DLS) method is used to set fair targets in rain-affected limited-overs matches.
explanation: DLS recalculates targets based on resources (overs and wickets) remaining when play is interrupted.

statement: Net Run Rate is commonly used as a tiebreaker for teams level on points in group-stage standings.
explanation: NRR compares scoring and conceding rates to separate teams with equal points.
